Hearing held (See reporter's transcript for full details). The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF TAL TAMIR, CELINE TAMIR Motion For Issue, Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions Against Lem Holdings, Llc, Ciaran Harty And Patrick Harty For Spoliation And Suppression Of Evidence is GRANTED.

The following facts are deemed established as issue sanctions:
(1) DD failed to preserve evidence as required and, in fact, improperly destroyed / spoliated such evidence, (2) the evidence improperly destroyed and otherwise withheld including, but not limited to, text messages and emails with each other, agents/brokers for prospective purchasers of the home (135 Yukon Street, San Francisco, CA), and others concerning, among other things, defects with the construction of the Home, water/flooding damage to the property, disclosures made to others and not Plaintiffs, the subject real estate purchase transaction as well as Defendant's knowledge of adverse facts and information concerning the home which it concealed from Plaintiffs, and (3) the trier of fact shall draw an inference that those documents and this information would have been unfavorable to Defendants.

Evidentiary sanctions granted and Defendants are barred from introducing any evidence or making any arguments in contravention of the foregoing issue sanctions. Monetary sanctions granted in the amount of $17,125 against Defendants jointly and severally to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of order.

Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Adjudication to the 11th cause of action of rescission is GRANTED as to the following: (1) Rescission of the Purchase Agreement; (2) Plaintiffs' return of the property (by way of rescinding deed) upon the return of the purchase funds by Defendant to Plaintiffs in the amount of $3,160,816.29; (3) An award of prejudgment interest in the amount of $729,997.02; (4) An award of consequential damages for funds paid by Plaintiffs to Defendant for improvements to the property in the amount of $93,5000; (5) An award of consequential damages for home loan (mortgage) payments, property taxes, and insurance incurred by Plaintiff in the amount of $557,664.20; (6) An award of CCP section 2394 punitive damages; and (7) an award of attorney fees to be established following order/judgment.

Plaintiffs did not waive their right to rescission, nor is their right to rescission estopped due to Plaintiffs delay in seeking the remedy.
